prose:
Frontier-orbital gap of benzene (HOMO–LUMO)
Method-provenance summary:
Property: HOMO–LUMO energy gap (Koopmans-style orbital difference) = 6.6310857 eV
Level of theory: DFT, tier "standard" → B3LYP/def2-TZVP with RI density fitting (PySCF v?.x)
Geometry provenance: single-point on the user-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz (no re-optimization)
Solvent/environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation model)
Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet, RHF/RKS reference)
Software: PySCF backend via chemkit frontier-orbitals skill; SCF converged in 8 cycles.
Convergence and warnings: SCF converged = true. The engine reported no warnings.
Integrity check: status "ok", trustworthy = true (all automated checks passed).

Additional numerical results (eV):
  HOMO energy  = –7.0468329
  LUMO energy  = –0.41574728
  HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.6310857 (headline value)

Reproducibility:
Command executed (from cli_invocation):
  chemkit_engine frontier --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --multiplicity 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp4g8a720i/live.json
The skill streamed its live log to a frontier_*.out file in the working directory while running (path echoed at launch); the final JSON result is at /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmp4g8a720i/live.json.

No warnings were issued by chemkit for this run.
